question.content: For 1 mol of a single-component, monatomic, non-relativistic van der Waals gas, the equation of state is (p+a/v^2)(v-b)=RT with a>0 and b>0. Consider the reversible Carnot cycle consisting of isothermal expansion at T_1 from v_1 to v_2, adiabatic expansion from (T_1,v_2) to (T_2,v_3), isothermal compression at T_2 from v_3 to v_4, and adiabatic compression back to (T_1,v_1). Determine only the cycle efficiency eta in terms of T_1 and T_2. Answer format: a single algebraic expression using only T_1 and T_2; write only the expression, with no explanatory text or equals sign.

step_1.content: From p=RT/(v-b)-a/v^2, the second T-derivative of p at fixed v is zero, so C_V is independent of v.
step_1.key_point: C_V depends only on T for this gas model.

step_3.content: Using dU=C_V dT+[T(partial p/partial T)_v-p]dv gives dU=C_V dT+a/v^2 dv, so deltaQ=C_V dT+RT/(v-b)dv.
step_3.key_point: Heat transfer separates into T-part and logarithmic v-part.

step_6.content: Integrating isotherms gives Q_1=RT_1 ln((v_2-b)/(v_1-b)) and Q_2=RT_2 ln((v_3-b)/(v_4-b)).
step_6.key_point: Both heats are logarithmic in shifted volume (v-b).

step_7.content: Adiabats satisfy integral(C_V(T)/T)dT+R ln(v-b)=const, leading to (v_2-b)/(v_1-b)=(v_3-b)/(v_4-b).
